page: Microsoft Azure L32aos_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16), AES cryptography, and SHA_NI.

The server is equipped with 256.0 GB of memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. A key hardware feature is its massive local storage capacity of 23,038 GB, which is Premium Storage capable. No dedicated GPU hardware is included in this configuration. This high storage-to-vCPU ratio and 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio position the server for data-intensive workloads requiring substantial local disk capacity and memory space.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3077.95 and a Geekbench single-core score of 2085.0. Multi-core performance is also robust, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 17812.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 196.26 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show average performance, with read-only large block speeds at 73.38 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 55.9 GB/sec. For database and web operations, the server achieves an average of 46.17 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 30.26 GB/sec. Large language model inference speeds on a small 135M model are strong, reaching 2623.93 tokens/sec for prompt processing, though performance drops to an average tier for larger models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the L32aos_v4 offers a high density of local storage and memory relative to its 32 vCPUs, making it a viable option for workloads that would otherwise require more expensive, higher-vCPU instances just to secure equivalent storage or RAM. The combination of massive local storage, 256 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance makes this server suitable for large-scale databases, data warehousing, heavy file processing, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure L32aos_v4 is an x86_64 server powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 3.7 GHz clock speed. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 256.0 GB of memory and a massive 23,038 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage, with no GPU. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2085.0, and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 196.26 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth and database operations, such as Redis SET performance at 46.17 million operations per second, fall into the average tier. Qualitatively, the server provides high storage and memory density relative to its compute resources, offering a cost-effective profile for storage-heavy and memory-intensive tasks. It is designed for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and heavy file processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L32aos_v4 is an x86_64 server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 23,038 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for database and storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L32aos_v4 features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 23,038 GB local storage, optimized for database and storage-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and massive local NVMe storage for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per physical core
Massive 23,038 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage
Strong single-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and heavy file processing
